canonical_definition: A psychometric method for evaluating creative products through holistic judgments by domain experts, adapted here for LLMs to assess poetry across criteria like creativity and quality.
papers: Enhancing Logical Reasoning in Language Models via Symbolically-GuidedMonteCarlo Process Supervision

definitions.text: A psychometric method for evaluating creative products through holistic judgments by domain experts, adapted here for LLMs to assess poetry across criteria like creativity and quality.
definitions.paper: Enhancing Logical Reasoning in Language Models via Symbolically-GuidedMonteCarlo Process Supervision

definitions.text: A psychometric method for evaluating creativity by aggregating judgments from multiple raters to establish a consensus score, adapted here for use with LLMs as raters.

relationships.paper: Enhancing Logical Reasoning in Language Models via Symbolically-GuidedMonteCarlo Process Supervision
relationships.evidence: The Consensual Assessment Technique (CAT) (Amabile, 1983) is a method for evaluating creative products by asking experts in the domain to provide their own holistic judgments of creativity, without being given explicit criteria to follow.

emnlp25: Enhancing Logical Reasoning in Language Models via Symbolically-GuidedMonteCarlo Process Supervision
